  "decision": "fail",

  "findings": [
    "Condenser pad not level — unit tilted, risking improper oil return to compressor and potential lubrication failure",
    "Pad setting does not meet manufacturer installation requirements for level placement"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Re-level condenser pad to within manufacturer tolerance (typically ±2° or 1/4 inch over pad width)",
    "Verify refrigerant lines and electrical connections are not stressed after re-leveling",
    "Confirm pad is properly supported on compacted base material to prevent future settling"
  ],
  "rationale": "A condenser pad that is not level can cause oil to pool in the compressor sump or refrigerant lines, preventing proper oil return to the compressor. This leads to lubrication starvation and premature compressor failure. Manufacturer installation manuals and IMC/IRC M1401 require equipment to be installed level per manufacturer instructions. The visible tilt indicates the pad was not properly set or has settled, making this a defective installation requiring correction before closeout.",
  "references": ["mfr install manual", "IMC M1401", "IRC M1401"]
}
